reason: Response B is more self-aware and layered: it does the meta-move of having the joke itself procrastinate, then breaks the fourth wall ("I was going to write a better one, but I figured I'd just do it tomorrow"), which reflects the wittier, more recursive style Opus tends toward. Response A is a clean, conventional setup-punchline that reads as the safer, more straightforward Sonnet approach. I'm only moderately confident since both are plausible, but B's extra self-referential layer tips me toward it being mine.
